children.title: 5.2.12.1.20 Command and Feature Lockdown (Log Page Identifier 14h)
children.description: This section defines the **Command Scope (CSP)** field within the **NVMe-MI Commands Supported and Effects Data Structure**, detailing how management interface operations map to specific architectural domains like the NVM Subsystem, Domain, Endurance Group, NVM Set, or Controller. It explains how to interpret and categorize command impact levels to determine whether an NVMe-MI command possesses global or localized subsystem scope.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.21 Boot Partition (Log Page Identifier 15h)
children.description: This section specifies the structure and attributes of the Command and Feature Lockdown Log Page (Identifier 15h) within the NVMe 2.3 specification. It defines the Command and Feature Identifier List Attributes (CFILA), detailing how the Contents Selected (CS) and Scope Selected (SS) bits categorize restricted command opcodes and Set Features identifiers for Admin queues or Management Endpoints.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.22 Rotational Media Information Log (Log Page Identifier 16h)
children.description: This section specifies the **Rotational Media Information Log (Log Page Identifier 16h)**, which tracks persistent media-related data for **Endurance Groups** utilizing rotational storage. It defines the logistical implementation of the **Get Log Page** command, detailing how to retrieve power-cycled data specific to an **Endurance Group Identifier**.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.23 Dispersed Namespace Participating NVM Subsystems (Log Page Identifier 17h)
children.description: This section defines the "Rotational Media Information" log page (Log Page Identifier 17h) within the NVM Express 2.3 specification. It details reliability telemetry for Endurance Groups, specifically tracking lifetime statistics for Failed Spinup Count, Load Count, and Failed Load Count to characterize mechanical actuator state transitions.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.24 Management Address List (Log Page Identifier 18h)
children.description: This section defines the **Management Address List (Log Page 18h)** in the NVMe 2.3 specification, detailing the structure of **Management Address Descriptors** used to identify system management communication endpoints. It answers how a host retrieves the association, type, and specific addresses of management controllers to facilitate out-of-band namespace and subsystem administration.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.25 Reachability Groups (Log Identifier 1Ah)
children.description: This section defines the **Reachability Groups log page (LID 1Ah)**, detailing how the host retrieves and indexes **Reachability Group descriptors** for namespaces attached to an **NVMe controller**. It specifies requirements for **Index Offset support**, **LPOL/LPOU field utilization**, and **Change Count validation** to ensure data consistency during multi-part log page transfers.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.26 Reachability Associations (Log Identifier 1Bh)
children.description: This section details the Reachability Associations Log (Log Identifier 1Bh) structure, covering the Reachability Groups Log Page layout, Reachability Group Descriptor format, fields such as RGID, NNID, CHNGC, and the ordering of Namespace Identifiers (NSID) within reachability groups, answering how controllers organize and report namespace reachability, change tracking, and group identification.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.27 Changed Allocated Namespace List (Log Identifier 1Ch)
children.description: The section details the Reachability Association Descriptor format for the “Changed Allocated Namespace List” log (Log ID 1Ch), covering fields such as Reachability Association ID (RASID), Number of RGID Values (NRID), Change Count (CHNGC), and Reachability Association Characteristics (RAC), and answers how these fields are encoded, when the RAO bit forces NRID to zero, how the 64‑bit Change Count increments, rolls over, or is cleared, and how hosts detect and interpret changes in reachability groups.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.28 Device Personalities (Log Page Identifier 1Dh)
children.description: This section details the **Device Personalities Log Page (1Dh)** of the NVMe specification, defining the structure of the **CDP Personality List** and the **Personality Properties** data structure. It answers technical questions regarding how controllers report supported system personalities, specifying log page header fields, versioning, and the mandatory ascending order of **Personality Identifier** values.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.29 Flexible Data Placement (FDP) Configurations (Log Page Identifier 20h)
children.description: Describes the Personality Properties data structure for Flexible Data Placement (FDP) configurations, covering Authenticated Unfreeze Support (AUS) fields, CDP Authentication Method Identifier, Programmable Key Authentication Support (PKAS), Physical Credential Authentication Support (PCAS), and Personality Attributes bits, and answers how to determine supported authentication methods and attribute flags for each personality identifier.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.30 Reclaim Unit Handle Usage (Log Page Identifier 21h)
children.description: This section details the bit-level structure of the Placement Identifier for Flexible Data Placement (FDP) configurations by defining the conditional field formatting based on Reclaim Group Identifier (RGIF) values. It specifies how the controller determines the Reclaim Unit Handle by parsing either a standalone Placement Handle or a composite field containing a Reclaim Group Identifier (RGID) and the associated Placement Handle.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.31 Flexible Data Placement (FDP) Statistics (Log Page Identifier 22h)
children.description: This section defines the Flexible Data Placement (FDP) Statistics log page (Identifier 22h) and the Reclaim Unit Handle Usage Descriptor for NVMe Endurance Groups. It specifies how FDP configuration lifetime data is tracked and details how Reclaim Unit Handles are categorized by host-specified or controller-specified attributes.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.32 Flexible Data Placement (FDP) Events (Log Page Identifier 23h)
children.description: This section details the Flexible Data Placement (FDP) Events log (Log Page 23h) in NVMe 2.3, covering Endurance Group identifiers, Reclaim Unit Handles, FDP event enablement, host vs. controller event types, log size (4 KiB), event retention policy, and the impact of firmware updates on log persistence. It answers how FDP events are recorded, filtered, and retrieved via Get Log Page, and which conditions cause event discarding or reporting.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.32.1 Host Events
children.description: This section covers NVM Express Host Event asynchronous event types related to Zoned Namespace (ZNS) management, specifically addressing reclaim unit operational statuses, time limit violations, reset recovery, and placement identifier validation. It provides administrative guidance for handling device-side reclamation errors, controller-level state modifications, and invalid zone placement requests.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.32.1.1 Reclaim Unit Not Fully Written To Capacity (Event Type 0h)
children.description: This section details the "Reclaim Unit Not Fully Written To Capacity" event (Type 0h) within the Zoned Namespaces (ZNS) specification, focusing on telemetry triggers, zone reclaim management, and error handling for underutilized capacity. It answers technical requirements regarding zone state transitions, diagnostic event reporting, and data integrity verification when a reclaim unit fails to reach its prescribed write limit.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.32.1.2 Reclaim Unit Time Limit Exceeded (Event Type 1h)
children.description: This section defines the **Reclaim Unit Time Limit Exceeded** event (Event Type 1h) within the storage management framework. It details technical parameters for identifying **latency threshold violations** during space reclamation operations and provides guidance for troubleshooting **background task timeouts** and **resource contention** issues.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.32.1.3 Controller Level Reset Modified Reclaim Unit Handles (Event Type 2h)
children.description: This section defines the Controller Level Reset Modified Reclaim Unit Handles event (Event Type 2h), detailing how NVMe-oF controllers communicate host-accessible memory reclaim operations following a reset. It addresses technical requirements for handle state consistency and synchronization protocols during controller-level recovery sequences.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.32.1.4 Invalid Placement Identifier (Event Type 3h)
children.description: This section defines the "Invalid Placement Identifier" FDP event (Type 03h), detailing the reporting requirements for the Reclaim Unit Handle Identifier (RUHID) and associated metadata fields. It specifies how controllers must signal the PIV, LV, and NSIDV bits when providing event-related context or indicating an inability to report specific Reclaim Unit identifiers.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.32.2 Controller Events
children.description: This section covers NVMe controller-generated asynchronous event notifications, specifically detailing Media Reallocated (80h) and Implicitly Modified Reclaim Unit Handle (81h) event types. It defines the technical telemetry and status triggers used by the controller to report changes in storage media health and the status of reclaim unit handles to the host.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.32.2.1 Media Reallocated (Event Type 80h)
children.description: This section defines the "Media Reallocated" Asynchronous Event Request (AER) (Event Type 80h) within the NVMe specification, specifically detailing the signaling mechanisms for notifying hosts about non-volatile memory block remapping. It addresses how the controller communicates internal wear-leveling or defect-management actions to the host to ensure data integrity and drive health monitoring.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.32.2.2 Implicitly Modified Reclaim Unit Handle (Event Type 81h)
children.description: This section specifies the data structures for the Implicitly Modified Reclaim Unit Handle (Event Type 81h) within the NVMe Flexible Data Placement (FDP) architecture, detailing field definitions for the Reclaim Unit Handle Identifier (RUHID) and Vendor Specific (VS) information. It explains the conditions for reporting Event Type 81h and outlines the requirements for validation bits (PIV/LV) when a controller cannot identify associated namespaces or reclaim units.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.33 Power Measurement (Log Page Identifier 25h)
children.description: This section defines the Power Measurement log page (Identifier 25h) within the NVMe 2.3 specification, detailing global NVM subsystem metrics including average/maximum interval power and power histogram distributions. It answers how controllers report persistent power consumption data, specifies the ordering requirements for histogram descriptors, and defines the structural criteria for interpreting power measurement counts and bin thresholds.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.34 Sanitize Namespace Status List (Log Page Identifier 7Fh)
children.description: This section defines the **Power Histogram Descriptor** format, specifying how to calculate **Power Histogram Bin Count (PHBC)** based on **Power Histogram Bin Lower Threshold (PHBLT)** and **Power Histogram Bin Size (PHBS)** metrics. It answers how NVM Express devices categorize and track cumulative power measurement intervals within specific wattage ranges during operation.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.35 Reservation Notification (Log Page Identifier 80h)
children.description: This section defines the **Reservation Notification Log Page (80h)** and the **Sanitize Namespace Status List** log parameters, specifying how controllers report sanitize progress and completion status via `NSID` filtering. It resolves technical requirements for interpreting `Sanitize State` values and error-handling protocols, specifically when a `Controller Pathing Error` interrupts data retrieval across domains.

children.title: 5.2.12.1.36 Sanitize Status (Log Page Identifier 81h)
children.description: This section details the **Reservation Notification Log Page (Identifier 81h)**, specifying the 64-bit **Log Page Count (LPC)** field and its behavior during queue overflows. It answers how controllers manage lost notifications, handle counter rollovers, and sequence unique event identifiers following a controller-level reset.

children.title: 5.2.12.2 Memory-Based Log Specific Information (PCIe)
children.description: This section details PCIe memory-based log structures, covering BAR configuration, memory-mapped register offsets, and log entry format requirements. It defines the implementation guidelines for hardware-to-system communication, enabling engineers to determine how to allocate memory resources and interpret standardized log data headers during PCIe error or status reporting.

children.title: 5.2.12.3 Message-Based Log Specific Information (Fabrics)
children.description: This section details fabric-specific message-based log pages, including Cross-Controller Reset, Lost Host Communication, and various Fabric Discovery mechanisms (including AVE and DDC Request logs). It addresses how fabric-attached NVMe controllers report state transitions, connectivity losses, and discovery-related management data to the host.

children.title: 5.2.12.3.1 Cross-Controller Reset (Log Identifier 1Eh)
children.description: This section defines the **Cross-Controller Reset (CCR) log page (Identifier 1Eh)**, detailing how an NVM subsystem tracks the status of both in-progress and completed reset operations initiated by a **Source Controller** against an **Impacted Controller (ICID)**. It identifies the regulatory conditions for logging these reset events and explains the notification mechanisms for fabrics-based **NVM Express (NVMe)** transport models.

children.title: 5.2.12.3.2 Lost Host Communication (Log Identifier 1Fh)
children.description: This section defines the **Lost Host Communication (Log Identifier 1Fh)**, specifying how an NVM subsystem monitors and reports secondary controllers that have unexpectedly lost host connectivity. It outlines technical criteria for identifying **Lost Host Communication (LHC) Controllers** and explains how hosts utilize this log to perform **Cross-Controller Reset Recovery**, evaluate **Time-Based Recovery**, and manage sub-system communication failures.

children.title: 5.2.12.3.3 Discovery (Log Page Identifier 70h)
children.description: This section defines the Discovery Log Page (Identifier 70h), detailing its exclusive use by NVMe Discovery controllers to provide persistent inventories of reachable NVM subsystems. It specifies implementation requirements for host-based association recovery and describes the LID Specific Parameter field used for filtering NVM subsystem port records.

children.title: 5.2.12.3.4 Host Discovery (Log Page Identifier 71h)
children.description: This section defines the structural metadata of the NVMe Discovery Log Page (71h), specifically detailing the `NUMREC`, `RECFMT`, and `DLPF` fields. It addresses how hosts identify the scope and formatting of discovery records, including whether entries are port-specific, global across the NVM subsystem, or extended in nature.

children.title: 5.2.12.3.5 AVE Discovery Log Page (Log Page Identifier 72h)
children.description: This section defines the AVE Discovery Log Page (72h) structure within the NVMe 2.3 specification, detailing header fields like Record Format (RECFMT) and flags, as well as the layout of the Host Extended Discovery Log Page entries. It answers questions regarding how log page length, host-specific reporting (ALLHOST), and the concatenated entry list are formatted for host discovery operations.

children.title: 5.2.12.3.6 Pull Model DDC Request Log Page (Log Page Identifier 73h)
children.description: This section details the Pull Model DDC Request Log Page (Log Page Identifier 73h) AVE Discovery Log Page structure—defining the Total Entry Length, AVE NQN (null‑terminated string), NUMATR (record count), and the sequential AVE Transport Record list (byte offsets, reserved fields, and record length)—and enumerates each AVE Transport Record’s fields (AVEADRFAM IPv4/IPv6 identifier, AVETRSVCID TCP port, AVETRADDR IP address) to answer how the log page data is formatted, parsed, and interpreted for NVMe‑OF AVE discovery.

children.title: 5.2.12.4 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the **Pull Model DDC Request log page** structure and **Command Specific Status values** for the **Get Log Page** command within the NVM Express 2.3 specification. It details the **Operation Request Identifier (ORI)**, **Operation Specific Parameters (OSP)**, and error handling codes, such as **Invalid Log Page (9h)**, to clarify how controllers process and report DDC management requests.

children.title: 5.2.13 Identify command
children.description: This section details the NVMe Identify command, covering common data structures, transport-specific implementations for PCIe and Fabrics, and command completion procedures. It serves as a technical reference for retrieving controller and namespace capabilities, host requirements, and supported feature sets.

children.title: 5.2.13.1 Identify command overview
children.description: This section defines the NVM Express "Pull Model DDC Request" log page, detailing the Operation Request Identifier (ORI) field, specific operation types (GAZ, AAZ, RAZ), and command completion status codes. It provides the technical structure for how a pull model DDC requests operations from a CDC and specifies how to interpret the associated Log Page responses and command-specific error conditions.

children.title: 5.2.13.2 Common Identify Data Structures
children.description: This section defines the NVMe Identify Controller and Namespace data structures, specifying Controller Structure (CNS) values for retrieving configuration parameters, namespace ID lists, and command set-specific capabilities. It serves as a technical reference for determining controller identity, namespace allocation status, and NVM subsystem topology through targeted host-to-controller identification queries.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.1 Identify Controller Data Structure (CNS 01h)
children.description: Identify Controller Data Structure (CNS 01h) details PCI Vendor ID, PCI Subsystem Vendor ID, Serial Number, controller capabilities, and feature flags; clarifies mandatory (M), optional (O5), and reserved (R) byte fields, mapping to NVMe Base Spec 2.3 sections and addressing how hosts query controller identity, interpret vendor identifiers, and construct subsystem discovery information.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.3 Namespace Identification Descriptor list (CNS 03h)
children.description: This section defines the **Namespace Identification Descriptor list (CNS 03h)** command in the **NVM Express 2.3 specification**, detailing the format and return requirements for **Namespace Identification Descriptor structures**. It addresses how a host queries active namespaces to obtain specific identification metadata by providing the required **NSID** input parameters and handling invalid identifier status codes.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.4 NVM Set List (CNS 04h)
children.description: This section defines the descriptors used in the Namespace Identification Descriptor structure, detailing the Namespace Identifier Type (NIDT), length (NIDL), and specific requirements for IEUID, NGUID, and NUUID identifiers. It answers how controllers must format unique namespace identification data and specifies the constraints for reporting these identifiers when corresponding Identify Namespace fields are unsupported.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.5 I/O Command Set specific Identify Namespace data structure (CNS 05h)
children.description: Section 5.2.13.2.5 defines the CNS 05h Identify Namespace data structure, detailing the NVM Set List layout (NUMENT header, up‑to‑31 entries), each NVM Set Attributes Entry fields (NSETID, ENDGID, reserved bytes, R4KRT, OWS), and explains how to parse entry counts, endurance group identification, typical 4 KiB random‑read latency, and optimal write‑size specifications.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.6 I/O Command Set specific Identify Controller data structure (CNS 06h)
children.description: This section defines the controller-level data structures returned for the NVMe I/O Command Set, addressing specific implementation details like command set identifiers, dataset management capabilities, and feature support. It answers how a host identifies the unique functional capabilities and I/O command set limitations of a controller when issuing a CNS 06h Identify command.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.7 I/O Command Set specific Active Namespace ID list (CNS 07h)
children.description: This section details the I/O Command Set specific Active Namespace ID list (CNS 07h), covering the retrieval of active namespace identifiers for specific I/O command sets. It defines the data structure format, controller behaviors, and command completion requirements for identifying active namespaces within a NVMe subsystem.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.8 I/O Command Set Independent Identify Namespace data structure (CNS 08h)
children.description: I/O Command Set‑specific identification (CNS 08h) – handling NSID = FFFFFFFFh, Namespace Management capability, CSI field selection

children.title: 5.2.13.2.9 Allocated Namespace ID list (CNS 10h)
children.description: This section defines the controller-level Identify Namespace List command (CNS 10h), detailing the data structure for retrieving identifiers of currently allocated namespaces. It specifies how NVMe controllers provide an ordered list of Namespace IDs (NSIDs) and clarifies the operational procedures for querying resource allocation status.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.10 Identify Namespace data structure for an Allocated Namespace ID (CNS 11h)
children.description: This section details the Identify Namespace data structure retrieved via Controller Identify (CNS 11h), covering specific fields for allocated namespaces within NVMe command sets. It answers questions regarding how to programmatically identify configuration settings, capacity, features, and capabilities of an existing namespace using the Identify command.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.11 Namespace Attached Controller list (CNS 12h)
children.description: This section defines the Namespace Attached Controller list retrieved via Controller Structure (CNS) 12h, detailing how the `NVME_List_Namespace_Attached_Controllers` structure identifies controllers attached to a specific namespace. It outlines the CNS 12h command format and data structure fields required to map controller identifiers to their associated namespaces within an NVMe subsystem.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.13 Namespace Granularity List (CNS 16h)
children.description: This section defines the Namespace Granularity List (CNS 16h) within the NVMe Identify Controller data structure, specifying the supported LBA size and namespace capacity increments. It answers how a controller communicates precise requirements for creating or resizing namespaces through granular alignment parameters for host-managed storage configurations.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.14 UUID List (CNS 17h)
children.description: This section details Identify command attributes for CNS 10h (Allocated Namespace ID list) and CNS 11h (Identify Namespace data structure), specifying retrieval requirements for NSIDs. It addresses how to programmatically query allocated namespace lists, retrieve namespace-specific metadata, and handle error conditions regarding unallocated or invalid NSIDs.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.15 Domain List (CNS 18h)
children.description: The section details the NVMe Identify Namespace (CNS 18h) Domain List structure, defining the 4 KB UUID

children.title: 5.2.13.2.16 Endurance Group List (19h)
children.description: This section defines the Endurance Group List structure (Log Page 19h) for NVMe NVM subsystems, detailing the format of Domain Attributes Entries including Domain Identifiers, Total Domain Capacity, and Max Endurance Group Domain Capacity. It addresses questions regarding how a controller reports endurance group topology, storage capacity allocation limits, and the specific data structures used to identify capacity within an NVMe domain.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.17 I/O Command Set specific Allocated Namespace ID list (CNS 1Ah)
children.description: This section defines the **Identify Controller** data structure for **I/O Command Set specific Allocated Namespace ID lists** using **Controller Namespace Structure (CNS) value 1Ah**. It details the retrieval of a list of active **Namespace IDs (NSIDs)** associated with a specific I/O Command Set, identifying which namespaces are currently allocated and valid for host access.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.18 I/O Command Set specific Identify Namespace data structure for an Allocated Namespace ID (CNS 1Bh)
children.description: This section defines the Identify Namespace data structure for allocated namespace identifiers using CNS 1Bh, detailing command-specific field requirements and data formatting for NVM subsystem reporting. It addresses how host software retrieves configuration parameters and identifying attributes for existing namespaces within specific I/O command sets.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.19 Identify I/O Command Set data structure (CNS 1Ch)
children.description: This section defines the "Identify I/O Command Set data structure" (CNS 1Ch) retrieval mechanism, specifically detailing how the Command Set Identifier (CSI) field dictates the return of I/O Command Set-specific namespace data. It addresses host queries regarding how to programmatically identify allocated namespaces and associated command sets within the NVM Express architecture.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.20 I/O Command Set Independent Identify Namespace data structure for an Allocated Namespace ID (CNS 1Fh)
children.description: This section defines the parameter requirements and data structure format for the Identify Namespace command with Controller Namespace Structure (CNS) value 1Fh. It addresses technical specifications for retrieving I/O Command Set Independent namespace attributes, facilitating host-side identification of allocated namespace identifiers within an NVMe storage environment.

children.title: 5.2.13.2.21 Supported Controller State Formats (CNS 20h)
children.description: Routing description: This section details the Identify I/O Command Set Data Structure (bytes 4088‑4095) and the I/O Command Set Vector format, enumer

children.title: 5.2.13.3 Memory-Based Transport Identify Data Structures (PCIe)
children.description: This section details PCIe memory-based transport Identify data structures, specifically covering Primary Controller Capabilities (CNS 14h) and Secondary Controller lists (CNS 15h). It defines the architectural parameters and data fields required to identify controller relationships and capabilities within a PCIe-based NVMe storage subsystem.

children.title: 5.2.13.3.1 Primary Controller Capabilities data structure (CNS 14h)
children.description: This section defines the "Primary Controller Capabilities" data structure, specifically detailing the versioning list for NVMe Controller State formats and the inclusion of Vendor Specific Controller State UUIDs. It addresses how a host identifies the supported structure versions and associated vendor-specific data formats for governing controller state reporting.

children.title: 5.2.13.3.2 Secondary Controller list (CNS 15h)
children.description: This section defines the Primary Controller Capabilities Structure for NVMe, specifically detailing Controller Resource Types (CRT) including VQ and VI resource support. It addresses how to identify flexible resource totals, current assignments to secondary controllers, and allocation status for the primary controller.

children.title: 5.2.13.4 Message-Based Transport Identify Data Structures (Fabrics)
children.description: This section defines the data structures for NVMe-over-Fabrics message-based transport, specifically detailing how to retrieve underlying namespace lists (CNS 1Dh) and port lists (CNS 1Eh). These specifications enable hosts to discover fabric-attached resources and enumerate transport-level connections within an NVMe subsystem.

children.title: 5.2.13.4.1 Get Underlying Namespace List (CNS 1Dh)
children.description: This section defines the "Get Underlying Namespace List" (CNS 1Dh) command structure, specifying the data format for the Secondary Controller List and individual Secondary Controller Entries. It details critical fields including Secondary Controller Identifier (SCID), Primary Controller Identifier (PCID), and the Online State (OLS) bit to explain how host software identifies and monitors the configuration of virtualized secondary controllers within an NVMe subsystem.

children.title: 5.2.13.4.2 Get Ports List (CNS 1Eh)
children.description: This section details the **Underlying Namespace List** data structure used by the **Get Ports List (CNS 1Eh)** command in the NVMe 2.3 specification. It defines the mapping of **Underlying NVM Subsystem NQNs**, **NSIDs**, and **Controller IDs (CNTLID)** to identify specific namespaces underlying a port.

children.title: 5.2.13.5 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines command execution protocols for the NVM Express® specification, specifically detailing the administrative completion requirements for Identify and Keep Alive commands. It clarifies controller responsibilities regarding Admin Completion Queue posting, Keep Alive Timer management, and introduces the Lockdown command for configuring system security capabilities.

children.title: 5.2.14 Keep Alive command
children.description: This section defines the Keep Alive command mechanism, specifying protocol-level heartbeat signaling, connection maintenance, and status verification requirements. It answers how systems validate active state synchronization, manage timeout thresholds, and handle command completion signaling within the communications architecture.

children.title: 5.2.14.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section outlines standard command completion requirements for NVMe Admin commands, specifically detailing the post-execution signaling behavior for Identify and Keep Alive commands within the Admin Completion Queue. It defines operational procedures for the Keep Alive Timer and provides the technical specifications for the Underlying Fabrics Transport Entry Data Structure.

children.title: 5.2.15 Lockdown command
children.description: This section details the parameters and implementation of the NVMe **Lockdown command**, which governs the **Command and Feature Lockdown** capability defined in section 8.1.5. It specifies how controllers should manage administrative restrictions to configure restricted access and security policies.

children.title: 5.2.15.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the completion behavior and error status codes for the NVMe Lockdown command, specifically addressing invalid prohibitions for opcodes or Feature Identifiers. It subsequently introduces the Migration Receive command, outlining its field requirements and the role of the Select field in managing migratable controllers.

children.title: 5.2.16 Migration Receive command
children.description: This section defines the **Migration Receive command** within NVMe 2.3, specifying how hosts utilize data structures via **Command Dwords** and the **Select field** to manage **migratable controllers**. It details the command's operational prerequisites and status reporting mechanisms required for facilitating controller migration.

children.title: 5.2.16.1 Migration Receive Management Operations
children.description: This section defines the migration receive management protocol, covering host-controller state synchronization and command completion procedures. It specifically details the implementation of "Get Controller State" operations (0h) required to manage firmware or state transitions during device migration.

children.title: 5.2.16.1.1 Get Controller State (Management Operation 0h)
children.description: This section details the Migration Receive command’s Dword fields (13–15)

children.title: 5.2.16.1.2 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines **NVMe Migration Receive command completion** requirements, including **controller state management** status codes like *Invalid Controller Identifier* and the *Controller Suspended (CSUP)* status bit. It provides technical specifications for interpreting **Admin Completion Queue** entries and initiates the functional overview for the **Migration Send command**.

children.title: 5.2.17 Migration Send command
children.description: This section defines the **Migration Send command** and its associated **Migration Receive** status codes within the **NVM Express 2.3** specification. It details command parameters, including **Command Dwords 10 and 14**, and clarifies status reporting for **controller migration and suspension states** managed through the **Admin Completion Queue**.

children.title: 5.2.17.1 Migration Send Management Operations
children.description: This section details Migration Send Management Operations, specifically covering **Suspend (0h)**, **Resume (1h)**, and **Set Controller State (2h)** commands within the migration data path. It defines the technical procedures for controlling state transitions and managing controller-level operations during active data migration processes.

children.title: 5.2.17.1.1 Suspend (Management Operation 0h)
children.description: This section details the Suspend management operation for the NVM Express Migration Send command, specifying control parameters for controller suspension and User Data Migration Queue (DUDMQ) management. It defines how to utilize Command Dword 11 to execute a Suspend Notification or a full suspension for a target controller identifier.

children.title: 5.2.17.1.3 Set Controller State (Management Operation 2h)
children.description: This section defines the operational constraints for NVMe controllers in a suspended state, specifically addressing how to restore functionality via the Migration Send Resume command. It covers technical behaviors regarding controller properties, Controller Memory Buffer (CMB) persistence, and Persistent Memory Region (PMR) availability during suspension.

children.title: 5.2.17.2 Command Completion
children.description: This section details the completion criteria for the NVMe Migration Send command, specifically addressing status codes, state verification, and recovery procedures for management operations. It answers questions regarding how the controller validates configuration fields (CSVI, CSUUIDI), handles SEQIND state commitments, and manages I/O queue creation following unsuccessful transfers.

children.title: 5.2.20 Namespace Attachment command
children.description: This section details the **Namespace Attachment** and **Migration Send** command operational requirements, specifically addressing validation criteria for **CSVI** and **CSUUIDI** fields. It defines error handling protocols for failed management operations and specifies the controller state commitment process for **I/O Submission and Completion Queues**.

children.title: 5.2.20.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section details NVMe namespace attachment rules, covering controller list processing, MAXDNA (Maximum Domain Namespace Attachments), MAXCNA (Maximum Controller Namespace Attachments), abort conditions for exceeding limits, and error codes for unsupported or disabled I/O Command Sets. It answers how a controller validates namespace attachment requests, when it aborts with “Namespace Attachment Limit Exceeded,” “I/O Command Set Not Supported,” or “I/O Command Set Not Enabled,” and how the Identify Controller data structure fields are used.

children.title: 5.2.21 Namespace Management command
children.description: This section defines the **Namespace Management command** parameters and **Namespace Attachment** error handling within the NVM Express 2.3 specification. It addresses the mechanics of namespace creation and deletion while identifying specific status codes—such as **ANA condition violations**, **I/O Command Set mismatches**, and **attachment limit overflows**—that dictate the success or failure of these administrative operations.

children.title: 5.2.21.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the NVM Express Namespace Management command parameters, including the Select (SEL) operation types, Command Set Identifier (CSI) configurations, and the structure of the data payload for namespace creation. It answers technical questions regarding how to initiate controller-level namespace provisioning and identifies the command-specific completion status codes, such as Invalid Format, returned by the Admin Completion Queue.

children.title: 5.2.22 Sanitize command
children.description: This section outlines the **Sanitize command** specifications, detailing **Namespace Management** command status values like **ANA Group Identifier Invalid** (24h) and **I/O Command Set Not Supported** (29h). It documents completion queue entry formats, specifically the **Namespace Identifier (NSID)**, and addresses how to initiate **NVM subsystem sanitize operations** or recover from previous sanitize failures.

children.title: 5.2.22.1 Command Completion
children.description: Sanitize‑Command Dword 10 details the NDAS (No‑Deallocate After Sanitize) and its interaction with the No‑Deallocate Inhibited bit, OIPBP (Overwrite Invert Pattern Between Passes), and OWPASS (Overwrite Pass Count) fields, defining when media deallocation occurs, when overwrite patterns are inverted, and how many overwrite passes are performed; it answers how the controller handles deallocation based on SANACT values, when the invert‑pattern option is applicable, and how the overwrite pass count is programmed.

children.title: 5.2.23 Sanitize Namespace command
children.description: This section details the **Sanitize Namespace command**, specifically regarding **Crypto Erase** operations and recovery from previous failures. It identifies **Command Specific Status Values** (such as firmware activation requirements, **Persistent Memory Region** conflicts, and migration suspensions) that prevent execution or indicate processing constraints.

children.title: 5.2.23.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the command completion behavior for NVM Express Sanitize Namespace operations, specifically detailing transitions between Media Verification and Post-Verification Deallocation states. It addresses how the controller validates the Enter Media Verification State (EMVS) bit, interacts with Crypto Erase (SANACT 100b) or Exit Media Verification (SANACT 101b) triggers, and handles error conditions like write protection or invalid state transitions.

children.title: 5.2.24 Security Receive command
children.description: The Security Receive command section details the protocol for retrieving status and data results generated by preceding Security Send commands within the NVMe specification. This documentation defines command-specific status criteria and operational mechanisms for managing asynchronous security data transfers between the host and the NVM subsystem.

children.title: 5.2.24.3 Security Protocol EAh
children.description: This section details the **Security Protocol EAh** specifications for **NVMe Security Receive and Send commands**, specifically defining the **Security Protocol Specific (SPSP) field** values—including **Replay Protected Memory Block (RPMB)** and **CDP Authentication**—to manage controller security interfaces. It answers how to structure command payloads for security discovery and target-specific authentication operations under the NVMe 2.3 base specification.

children.title: 5.2.25 Security Send command
children.description: Security Send command usage, NVMe security protocols (00h, EAh), Allocation Length field definition, Security Protocol Specific (SPSP)

children.title: 5.2.25.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section details the NVM Express Security Send command structure, specifically defining parameter mappings for Command Dwords 10 and 11, including Security Protocol (SECP), SP Specific fields, and Transfer Length (TL). It outlines requirements for Admin Completion Queue status reporting and introduces the Set Features command’s reliance on specific Data Pointers and Command Dwords.

children.title: 5.2.26 Set Features command
children.description: This section defines the **Set Features** command structure, detailing its reliance on **Data Pointer**, **Command Dword 10**, and **Command Dword 14** for configuring NVM Express controller attributes. It clarifies the implementation requirements for security protocol parameters within the command format while addressing how the controller executes and completes feature configuration requests.

children.title: 5.2.26.1 Common Feature Specific Information
children.description: This section defines the metadata parameters for NVMe Set Features command identifiers, specifically addressing persistence, data buffer usage, scope, and vendor-specific behavior. It answers how controller configurations are maintained across power cycles, whether a Host Memory Buffer requires a data payload, and how feature settings are scoped across NVM subsystem controllers.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.2 Power Management (Feature Identifier 02h)
children.description: This section defines the NVM Express Power Management feature, detailing Power State management, autonomous power state transitions, and latency tolerance reporting. It specifies how hosts configure thermal management and power consumption thresholds to optimize device performance and energy efficiency.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.3 Temperature Threshold (Feature Identifier 04h)
children.description: This section defines the NVM Express Arbitration Feature (01h), detailing configuration parameters for command arbitration including High, Medium, and Low Priority Weights and the Arbitration Burst limit. It addresses how to manage controller command execution scheduling and per-Submission Queue fetch limits using Command Dword 11 attributes.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.3.1 Temperature Threshold Hysteresis
children.description: This section details NVMe temperature monitoring, covering Composite Temperature, up to nine sensor readings, over‑temperature and under‑temperature thresholds,

children.title: 5.2.26.1.4 Volatile Write Cache (Feature Identifier 06h)
children.description: This section specifies the NVM Express mechanism for managing volatile write cache feature settings, specifically detailing the configuration of temperature threshold hysteresis via the Set and Get Features commands. It defines technical parameters such as Temperature Threshold Hysteresis (TMPTHH) and Threshold Type Select (THSEL) to dictate how controllers handle temperature-related status transitions and error reporting requirements.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.5 Asynchronous Event Configuration (Feature Identifier 0Bh)
children.description: This section details the **Asynchronous Event Configuration (Feature Identifier 0Bh)**, which allows hosts to define specific controller events that trigger asynchronous event notifications. It explains how to manage persistent condition reporting and provides the bit-level structure for configuring these notifications via **Command Dword 11**.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.6 Autonomous Power State Transition (Feature Identifier 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the Autonomous Power State Transition feature (Feature Identifier 0Ch), describing the Dword‑11 layout for asynchronous event configuration—including the Attached Namespace Attribute Notices (bit 8) and SMART/Health Critical Warnings field (bits 7‑0)—and explains how hosts enable or suppress event notifications based on log‑page status and controller behavior. It answers how to configure APP and SHCW bits, what conditions trigger asynchronous events, and how the controller’s power‑state transition settings interact with the SMART/Health Information log.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.7 Timestamp (Feature Identifier 0Eh)
children.description: This section defines the **Autonomous Power State Transition (APST)** feature, detailing the **Idle Time Prior to Transition (ITPT)** and **Idle Transition Power State (ITPS)** bit fields within the APST data structure. It addresses how NVMe controllers autonomously transition to non-operational power states and clarifies technical interactions between **APSTE** and the **Non-Operational Power State Config (NOPPME)** feature.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.8 Keep Alive Timer (Feature Identifier 0Fh)
children.description: Section 5.2.26.1.8 details the Keep‑Alive Timer (Feature 0Fh) in NVMe 2.3, covering Timestamp format (48‑bit milliseconds since 01‑Jan‑1970 UTC), Set Features and Get Features data structures, Timestamp Origin handling (reset‑relative vs. host‑set), overflow behavior, and controller reset effects; it answers how the controller exposes, updates, and interprets timestamps, what values are returned after a reset, and how hosts compute elapsed time for keep‑alive operations.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.9 Host Controlled Thermal Management (Feature Identifier 10h)
children.description: This section defines the **Host Controlled Thermal Management (HCTM)** feature (Identifier 10h), detailing the use of **Command Dword 11** to configure controller-level thermal threshold settings. It specifies technical implementation requirements for managing device temperatures and provides the necessary structure for retrieving these thermal parameters via **Get Features** commands.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.10 Non-Operational Power State Config (Feature Identifier 11h)
children.description: This section defines the Non-Operational Power State Configuration feature (11h), specifying parameters for power state entry, exit latency, and transition behaviors. It addresses how NVMe controllers manage energy consumption during idle periods and configures transition thresholds between operational and non-operational states.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.11 Read Recovery Level Config (Feature Identifier 12h)
children.description: This section defines the **Read Recovery Level Config** (Feature Identifier 12h) within the **NVMe Base Specification**, detailing controller-level settings for error recovery strategies. It clarifies how controllers manage read operations and associated recovery parameters to maintain data integrity during command execution.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.12 Predictable Latency Mode Config (Feature Identifier 13h)
children.description: This section defines the configuration of NVM Sets for Predictable Latency Mode, detailing the use of Feature Identifier 13h, Command Dwords, and the Deterministic Threshold Configuration data structure. It addresses how to enable the mode, manage background operation delays, handle warning thresholds, and transition controllers into the Non-Deterministic Window.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.13 Predictable Latency Mode Window (Feature Identifier 14h)
children.description: This section details the Predictable Latency Mode Window (Feature 14h), covering NVM subsystem configuration, Deterministic Window (DTWIN) operational states, and latency constraints. It addresses how to manage window timing parameters, execute window transitions, and verify deterministic performance requirements for predictable I/O throughput.

children.description: Section 5.2.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.15 Sanitize Config (Feature Identifier 17h)
children.description: This section defines the "Sanitize Config" feature (17h) within the NVM Express specification, focusing on the Host Behavior Support data structure's Copy Descriptor Formats Enable (CDFE) field. It details the specific bit-mapping used to enable or disable individual Copy Descriptor Formats (2h through 4h) based on controller support levels.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.16 Endurance Group Event Configuration (Feature Identifier 18h)
children.description: This section defines the **Endurance Group Event Configuration** (Feature 18h) within the NVMe specification, detailing how to manage asynchronous event triggers and notification masks for endurance group status changes. It addresses how hosts configure threshold warning alerts and event reporting mechanisms for managing device wear-out and reliability states.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.18 Spinup Control (Feature Identifier 1Ah)
children.description: This section defines the **Spinup Control Feature (1Ah)** within the ATA command set, detailing power-on spinup management, the **PUIS (Power-Up In Standby)** feature, and the **SET FEATURES** command. It addresses how to configure drive spin-up behavior, manage operational power states, and implement diagnostic spin-up sequences for energy-efficient disk drive integration.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.19 Power Loss Signaling Config (Feature Identifier 1Bh)
children.description: This section defines the **Power Loss Signaling Config** (Feature ID 1Bh) and **Spinup Control** (Feature ID 1Ah), detailing how to manage **I/O Command Set Combination Indices** and rotational media spinup configurations within **NVM Express 2.3**. It addresses how hosts configure operational command sets and handle initial spinup requirements for Endurance Groups.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.20 Flexible Data Placement (Feature Identifier 1Dh)
children.description: This section defines the **Flexible Data Placement (FDP)** operation via **Feature Identifier 1Dh**, detailing how to manage the feature within specific **Endurance Groups (ENDGID)** using **Command Dword 11 and 12**. It addresses how to programmatically enable or disable FDP capabilities and retrieve status attributes through **Get Features** and **Set Features** commands within the NVMe 2.3 specification.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.21 Flexible Data Placement Events (Feature Identifier 1Eh)
children.description: This section details the Flexible Data Placement (FDP) Events feature (Identifier 1Eh), covering the control and retrieval of event types associated with specific Reclaim Unit Handles. It defines constraints for enabling or disabling FDP events, specifies error handling for invalid command parameters, and describes the data buffer structure for Get/Set Feature commands.

children.description: Section 5.2.26.1.22 explains the Namespace Admin Label (Feature Identifier

children.title: 5.2.26.1.23 Controller Data Queue (Feature Identifier 21h)
children.description: This section defines the Controller Data Queue (CDQ) Feature (Identifier 21h) within the NVMe specification, detailing mechanisms for managing head pointer synchronization and enabling tail pointer trigger events. It provides technical guidance on configuring CDQID parameters and event notification logic to facilitate efficient host-to-controller data communication.
