children.description: This section details the Configurable Device Personality (Feature Identifier 22h

children.title: 5.2.26.1.24.1 Authentication Methods
children.description: This section details high-security access control verification protocols, specifically covering Physical Credential and Programmable Key authentication modules. It addresses how hardware-based tokens and encrypted electronic keys validate user identity within authentication frameworks.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.24.1.1 Physical Credential Authentication
children.description: This section outlines requirements for Physical Credential Authentication, covering identity verification protocols, cryptographic binding, and secure token validation. It defines technical standards for smart card readers, biometric integration, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) processes to ensure secure physical access control.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.24.1.2 Programmable Key Authentication
children.description: This section details Programmable Key Authentication and Physical Credential Authentication as optional methods for unfreezing NVM subsystem personalities via Security Send and Receive commands. It defines implementation requirements for the Authenticated Unfreeze Support (AUS) feature, specifying how hosts use vendor-defined indicators or host-programmable keys to manage personality state transitions.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.24.3 Security Personality
children.description: This section defines the **Manufacturing Default Personality** mechanism within the **NVMe 2.3 specification**, detailing how the **Set Features command** resets controller settings and **Configurable Device Personality (CDP)** attributes to factory states. It clarifies requirements for the **Personality Manufacturing Default Settings State (PMDSS)** bit and explains the scope of changes affecting both visible feature values and internal vendor-specific configurations.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.25 Power Limit (Feature Identifier 23h)
children.description: This section defines the **Security Personality Attributes Data Structure** within the NVMe 2.3 specification, detailing how bitmasks control the **prohibited or allowed status** of specific security protocols, including **Vendor Specific Protocols (F0h-F1h)** and **AHATSDP (EEh)**. It answers how host systems programmatically configure security protocol enforcement and policy updates via the **ASP (Allowed/Prohibited)** bit.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.26 Power Threshold (Feature Identifier 24h)
children.description: This section defines the Power Threshold feature (Identifier 24h), detailing the management of TPER (Thermal Power Exceedance Reporting) and power consumption limits. It specifies implementation requirements for setting power thresholds and handling associated asynchronous event notifications in NVMe-compliant devices.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.27 Power Measurement (Feature Identifier 25h)
children.description: Power Measurement (Feature 25h) – defines PLV/PLS fields in Command Dword 13, interaction with Set / Get Features, validation against Maximum Power (MP) in Power State Descriptors (PSD) and Non‑Operational State (NOPS) bits, and error handling via “Invalid Power Limit”. Power Threshold (Feature 24h) – describes configuration via Command Dword 11, saveable attribute semantics, default setting, and controller reporting of Power Threshold Exceeded events.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.28 Embedded Management Controller Address (Feature Identifier 78h)
children.description: This section defines the Embedded Management Controller Address (Feature Identifier 78h) within the MCTP base specification. It specifies device-addressing protocols, identifies mandatory fields for management controller communication, and details how the system determines correct routing for MCTP messages.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.29 Host Management Agent Address (Feature Identifier 79h)
children.description: This section defines the Host Management Agent Address feature (79h) within the NVMe specification, detailing the Data Structure for identifying the management agent's transport address. It answers how a host establishes communication with out-of-band management entities using specific NVMe-MI transport protocols and address formats.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.30 Host Metadata (Feature Identifier 7Dh), (Feature Identifier 7Eh), (Feature Identifier 7Fh)
children.description: This section specifies the protocol for configuring the Embedded Management Controller Address (Feature Identifier 78h) via Set and Get Features commands using a URI-formatted data structure. It addresses how to establish a communication path between the NVM subsystem and external system management agents, such as enclosure managers or management controllers.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.30.1  Enhanced Controller Metadata (Feature Identifier 7Dh)
children.description: This section defines the **Host Metadata data structure** and the **Metadata Element Descriptor** format for **Enhanced Controller Metadata (Feature Identifier 7Dh)** within the NVMe 2.3 specification. It specifies the configuration for passing multiple metadata elements to a controller, detailing the **Number of Metadata Element Descriptors (NMED)** field and the allowable density of element types.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.30.2 Controller Metadata (Feature Identifier 7Eh)
children.description: This section defines the Controller Metadata feature (Identifier 7Eh), specifying a structured list of UTF-8 encoded element types for host-to-controller reporting. It provides the technical specifications required to answer how host environment details—such as OS attributes, driver versions, processor models, and firmware—are transmitted to an NVMe controller.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.30.3 Namespace Metadata (Feature Identifier 7Fh)
children.description: Namespace Metadata (Feature Identifier 7Fh) – retrieval of controller‑specific, namespace‑specific metadata via Get Features (SEL = 011b), interpretation of Command Dword 11 and Host Metadata structure, NS‑Specific completion‑queue flag, and sanitize‑operation impact (Fig 704); Software Progress Marker (Feature Identifier 80h) – persistent OS‑load status reporting via Get Features, with Command Dword 11 fields (Pre‑boot Software Load Count, reserved bits) and corresponding completion‑queue return (Fig 465).

children.title: 5.2.26.1.31 Software Progress Marker (Feature Identifier 80h)
children.description: This section defines the **Software Progress Marker (Feature 80h)**, detailing the specific register format, bit-masking requirements, and operational behavior for host-to-controller status reporting. It addresses how to implement and interpret progress markers to track initialization, configuration, or firmware-specific execution states within the NVM Express subsystem.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.32 Host Identifier (Feature Identifier 81h)
children.description: This section defines the **Host Identifier (Feature Identifier 81h)** and **Software Progress Marker (Feature Identifier 80h)** within the NVMe 2.3 specification, detailing how to use persistent markers for OS boot monitoring. It addresses technical requirements for retrieving command attributes, managing namespace-specific metadata, and understanding how sanitize operations impact these persistent controller features.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.32.2 NVMe over Fabrics Implementations
children.description: This section defines Host Identifier requirements for PCIe and NVMe over Fabrics (NVMe-oF) transport implementations, specifying mandatory 128-bit support for NVMe-oF and reset protocols for legacy PCIe values. It serves as a technical reference for controller configuration standards regarding host identification and reservation notification masking.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.33 Reservation Notification Mask (Feature Identifier 82h)
children.description: This section defines the Reservation Notification Mask feature (82h), detailing the bit-field configurations used by the host to enable or disable specific reservation-related event notifications within NVM subsystems. It identifies which asynchronous events trigger notification commands and explains the mandatory implementation requirements for controller-level reservation reporting.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.34 Reservation Persistence (Feature Identifier 83h)
children.description: This section defines implementation requirements for the **Host Identifier** feature across PCIe and NVMe over Fabrics transport layers, alongside protocol specifications for the **Reservation Notification Mask**. It addresses how host identifiers must be configured or reset upon controller initialization and determines which reservation events trigger log page generation.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.35 Namespace Write Protection Config (Feature Identifier 84h)
children.description: This section defines the Namespace Write Protection Config (Feature Identifier 84h), detailing how Persist‑Through‑Power‑Loss (PTPL) is enabled or disabled for reservation‑capable namespaces via Set Features and Get Features commands, the handling of namespace IDs (including FFFFFFFFh), error conditions (Invalid Field in Command), and the non‑saveable nature of the feature. It also specifies the format of the returned Dword 0 in the completion queue entry, answering how PTPL state is queried, modified, and reported for supported namespaces.

children.title: 5.2.26.1.36 Boot Partition Write Protection Config (Feature Identifier 85h)
children.description: This section defines the **Boot Partition Write Protection Config (Feature Identifier 85h)**, detailing how the host manages write protection states for primary and secondary Boot Partitions via **Set Features** commands. It provides technical specifications for governing state transitions, default configurations, and operational persistence within the **NVM Express** architecture.

children.title: 5.2.26.2 Memory-Based Transport Feature Specific Information (PCIe)
children.description: Section 5.2.26.2 defines technical parameters for PCIe memory-based transport, specifically detailing queue allocation, interrupt coalescing, vector configuration, and host memory buffer management. This section serves as a reference for configuring performance-critical transport features and identifying the operational limits of the host-to-controller interface.

children.title: 5.2.26.2.1 Number of Queues (Feature Identifier 07h)
children.description: This section defines the **Boot Partition Write Protection Config** feature (Identifier 07h) within the NVMe specification, detailing operational states such as **Write Unlocked**, **Write Locked**, and **Write Locked Until Power Cycle**. It specifies command constraints, including status codes for invalid state transitions and the interaction between **Set Features** commands, **RPMB** control, and power-cycle requirements.

children.title: 5.2.26.2.2 Interrupt Coalescing (Feature Identifier 08h)
children.description: This section specifies NVMe queue‑allocation fields—NSQR (Number of I/O Submission Queues Requested), NCQA (Number of I/O Completion Queues Allocated), and NSQA (Number of I/O Submission Queues Allocated)—detailing valid ranges, zero‑based indexing, maximum limits, virtualized allocation‑unit behavior, and error handling for invalid request values. It answers how hosts request, how controllers allocate, and what constraints and status codes apply to I/O queue configuration.

children.title: 5.2.26.2.3 Interrupt Vector Configuration (Feature Identifier 09h)
children.description: This section details the definition and enabling of interrupt vectors for specific functional units, focusing on **Feature Identifier 09h**, **Interrupt Input Pins**, and **Vector Mapping registers**. It answers how to programmatically assign hardware interrupt sources to system-level service routines and configure the signaling parameters required for proper event handling.

children.title: 5.2.26.2.4 Host Memory Buffer (Feature Identifier 0Dh)
children.description: This section covers the **Host Memory Buffer (Feature Identifier 0Dh)** and **Interrupt Vector Configuration (Feature Identifier 09h)** within the NVM Express 2.3 specification. It defines parameters for **Interrupt Coalescing** thresholds and outlines the specific requirements for configuring controller **Interrupt Vectors** associated with **I/O Completion Queues**.

children.title: 5.2.26.3 Message-Based Transport Feature Specific Information (Fabrics)
children.description: This section details the Host Memory Buffer (HMB) Descriptor Entry structure, including Buffer Size (BSIZE) and Buffer Address (BADD) specifications. It clarifies how controllers process memory page-aligned buffers and defines the Host Memory Non-operational Access Restricted (HMNAR) attribute returned during Get Features commands.

children.title: 5.2.26.3.1 Asynchronous Event Configuration (Feature Identifier 0Bh) for Fabrics
children.description: This section clarifies that the Asynchronous Event Configuration (Feature Identifier 0Bh) is not Fabrics-specific, redirecting users to section 5.2.26.1.5 for relevant event notification protocols. It details the Command Completion requirements for the Set Features command and defines status-specific error codes for feature modifications, saveability, and namespace scoping.

children.title: 5.2.26.4 Command Completion
children.description: This section details the command completion requirements for the NVMe `Set Features` command, including prerequisites for posting entries to the Admin Completion Queue and a specification of command-specific status codes (0Dh–15h). It addresses how controllers handle errors related to feature saveability, changeability, namespace scope, and invalid command set combinations.

children.title: 5.2.27 Track Receive command
children.description: This section details the **Set Features command** completion process and error handling within the **NVMe Base Specification**, specifically addressing **Command Specific Status Values** for non-saveable, non-changeable, or incompatible features. It answers questions regarding controller behavior upon command completion and how to interpret specific status codes like **Feature Identifier Not Saveable (0Dh)** or **I/O Command Set Combination Rejected (15h)**.

children.title: 5.2.27.1 Track Receive Management Operations
children.description: Section 5.2.27.1 details the mechanisms for monitoring memory state transitions and managing command execution acknowledgments using Track Receive Management Operations. It addresses technical requirements for tracking memory changes (Operation 0h) and protocols for verifying successful command completion in system synchronization.

children.title: 5.2.27.1.1 Tracked Memory Changes (Management Operation 0h)
children.description: This section specifies the **Track Receive** command operation **0h (Tracked Memory Changes)** for retrieving host memory modification logs documented in the **Tracked Memory Change data structure**. It defines how to utilize **Command Dword 12 (NUMDL)** and **Command Dword 11 (CNTLID)** to query specific controller activity and retrieve NVM Express host memory tracking telemetry.

children.title: 5.2.27.1.2 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the completion procedures and error status codes for the Track Receive command, alongside the command structure for the Track Send management operation. It addresses how controllers report memory tracking conflicts and specifies the Command Dword 10 field requirements for initiating management operations in an NVM subsystem.

children.title: 5.2.28 Track Send command
children.description: This section details the **Track Send** command, focusing on **management operations** and **command completion protocols** within the system architecture. It addresses how to initiate track transmissions, define operational parameters, and interpret status codes returned upon process execution.

children.title: 5.2.28.1 Track Send Management Operations
children.description: This section outlines track send management operations, detailing protocols for logging user data modifications and monitoring memory changes. It addresses how to execute and audit system-level tracking configurations using specific management operation codes (0h and 1h).

children.title: 5.2.28.1.1 Log User Data Changes (Management Operation 0h)
children.description: This section defines the "Log User Data Changes" management operation (0h) within the NVMe Track Send command, detailing Command Dword 10 configuration and the Track Receive completion status codes. It answers how controllers manage host memory change tracking and provides status definitions for invalid controller identifier errors.

children.title: 5.2.28.1.2 Track Memory Changes (Management Operation 1h)
children.description: This section defines the "Log User Data Changes" management operation (1h), specifying how to start or stop tracking user data modifications by configuring the Controller Data Queue Identifier (CDQID). It addresses how controllers initiate audit trails for NVMe namespace changes and the corresponding Command Dword 11 interface requirements.

children.title: 5.2.28.2 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines command completion requirements for the "Track Memory Changes" command, specifically detailing the Memory Range Tracking Granularity (MRTG) and the Number of Memory Range Tracking Descriptors (NMRTD). It addresses controller behavior regarding host memory tracking, undefined states for overlapping memory regions (PMR/CMB), and potential conflicts with MSI/MSI-X register address ranges.

children.title: 5.3 Memory-Based Transport Admin Commands (PCIe)
children.description: This section details the **Track Memory Changes** command within the NVM Express PCIe memory-based transport, outlining constraints for host memory address ranges, MSI/MSI-X register handling, and potential undefined behaviors. It addresses key technical specifications for **Memory Range Tracking Granularity (MRTG)** and the **Number of Memory Range Tracking Descriptors (NMRTD)**, defining the limits and reporting capabilities of the controller when monitoring memory modifications.

children.title: 5.3.1 Create I/O Completion Queue command
children.description: This section details the parameters and requirements for the **Create I/O Completion Queue** command in NVM Express, specifically addressing memory allocation via **PRP Entries** and **PRP Lists**. It answers how to specify a communication queue's base memory address and the host’s obligations regarding memory persistence and alignment during the queue’s lifecycle.

children.title: 5.3.1.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the controller-side acknowledgement process for "Create I/O Completion Queue" commands, specifically detailing error status codes for invalid queue identifiers, queue sizing, and interrupt vectors. It provides the technical criteria for command failure and outlines the initialization requirements necessary for establishing I/O submission queues.

children.title: 5.3.2 Create I/O Submission Queue command
children.description: This section defines the **Create I/O Submission Queue** and **Create I/O Completion Queue** commands within the NVMe 2.3 specification, detailing status codes for errors like **Invalid Queue Identifier**, **Invalid Queue Size**, and **Invalid Interrupt Vector**. It explains the regulatory constraints for queue creation, including controller-supported limits (CAP.MQES) and mandatory initialization of the **CC.IOCQES** field.

children.title: 5.3.2.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the configuration parameters for the **Create I/O Submission Queue** command, specifically detailing **Completion Queue Identifier (CQID)** validation, **Queue Priority (QPRIO)** arbitration settings, and the **Physically Contiguous (PC)** bit. It answers how a host associates a Submission Queue with a specific Completion Queue and how to assign priority classes and memory contiguity attributes during queue creation.

children.title: 5.3.3 Delete I/O Completion Queue command
children.description: This section details the Delete I/O Completion Queue command, focusing on controller-to-host interface requirements, completion queue deletion procedures, and associated command status results. It addresses requirements for successful deletion, handling active queue state, and defining the resulting behavior for associated submission queues.

children.title: 5.3.3.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section details command completion status values for the Create I/O Submission Queue command and specifies requirements for the Delete I/O Completion Queue command. It defines error conditions, such as invalid queue identifiers or sizes, and mandates the deletion of associated Submission Queues prior to Completion Queue removal.

children.title: 5.3.4 Delete I/O Submission Queue command
children.description: Delete I/O Submission Queue command: defines command‑specific status values (Completion Queue Invalid, Invalid Queue Identifier, Invalid Queue Size) and explains error conditions for invalid queue identifiers, sizes, and uninitialized IOSQES. Delete I/O Completion Queue command: describes required use of Command Dword 10, PRP List deallocation, prerequisite deletion of associated Submission Queues, and the resulting Invalid Queue status when those prerequisites are not met.

children.title: 5.3.4.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section details the protocol for deleting I/O Submission Queues within the NVMe architecture, covering mandatory command completion behaviors and the implicit abortion of pending I/O requests. It provides technical specifications for the Delete I/O Submission Queue command, including Queue Identifier usage, doorbell synchronization, and error handling for invalid identifiers.

children.title: 5.3.5 Doorbell Buffer Config command
children.description: This section details the **Doorbell Buffer Config command** within the NVMe 2.3 specification, covering the configuration of doorbell shadow registers and event indices to optimize host-controller communication. It addresses how to establish a memory-backed doorbell buffer mechanism to minimize PCIe register-read latency and memory overhead.

children.title: 5.3.5.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section details NVMe command‑completion mechanisms, covering Shadow Doorbell and EventIdx buffer configuration (offset calculations, PRP1/PRP2 base addresses

children.title: 5.3.6 Virtualization Management command
children.description: This section details the **Virtualization Management command** for **Doorbell Buffer Configuration**, specifically defining memory addressing requirements for **Shadow Doorbell** and **EventIdx** buffers using **PRP1** and **PRP2** pointers. It clarifies the calculation of buffer offsets based on **CAP.DSTRD** and specifies the controller's behavioral requirements for handling invalid memory address pointers.

children.title: 5.3.6.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the virtualization‑management Command Dword 10 layout—Controller Identifier (CNTLID), Resource Type (RT), and Action (ACT)—and explains how to modify controller resources via actions such as Primary Controller Flexible Allocation (ACT = 1h) and Secondary Controller Offline (ACT = 7h), detailing valid field values, persistence across resets, and error handling for Invalid Controller Identifier.

children.title: 5.4 Message-Based Transport Admin Commands (Fabrics)
children.description: Section 5.4 details message-based transport administration commands for NVMe-over-Fabrics architectures, covering configuration, management, and security tasks like exported NVM subsystem provisioning, zoning, and discovery log management. These specifications define the command structures and operational procedures required to manage exported namespaces, ports, and cross-controller resets within NVMe fabric environments.

children.title: 5.4.1 Clear Exported NVM Resource Configuration command
children.description: This section defines the "Clear Exported NVM Resource Configuration" command, detailing the operational requirements and parameters for resetting non-volatile memory (NVM) export states. It addresses the protocol-specific command structure and execution criteria necessary to ensure successful controller-to-host resource de-provisioning.

children.title: 5.4.1.1 Command Completion
children.description: Section 5.4.1.1 details NVMe virtualization‑management command completion, enumerating status values 1Fh (Invalid Controller Identifier), 20h (Invalid Secondary Controller State), 21h (Invalid Number of Controller Resources), 22h (Invalid Resource Identifier), and defines Completion Queue Dword 0 fields—reserved bits 31:16 and 15:0 Number of Controller Resources Modified (NRM). It answers which error codes signal malformed controller or resource requests, how to decode the Dword 0 payload, and how NRM reflects allocated flexible resources during primary/secondary controller actions.

children.title: 5.4.2 Create Exported NVM Subsystem command
children.description: This section defines the "Create Exported NVM Subsystem" command, detailing specific Virtualization Management completion queue error codes—such as invalid controller identifiers and resource limitations—and the structure of Dword 0 for reporting modified controller resources. It provides technical specifications for managing secondary controller states and verifying flexible resource allocations within an NVM subsystem.

children.title: 5.4.2.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section details the parameters and completion requirements for the **Create Exported NVM Subsystem** administrative command, covering the **Data Pointer**, **Restricted Access (RA)** bit, and **Command Dword 10** fields. It specifies how the controller initializes an exported subsystem, enforces **Host NQN** access controls, and returns the **SUBNQN** to the host.

children.title: 5.4.3 Cross-Controller Reset command
children.description: This section details the **Create Exported NVM Subsystem** command, specifying **Data Pointer** configurations, **Restricted Access (RA)** bit functionality, and **NVM Subsystem** initialization requirements. It addresses how to programmatically instantiate an **Exported NVM Subsystem**, define host access policies, and retrieve the **SUBNQN** upon successful command completion.

children.title: 5.4.3.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the Command Completion requirements for the NVMe Cross-Controller Reset, detailing the CQE Dword 0 fields including Immediate Reset Successful (IRS), Validated (V), and Controller Level Reset Initiated (CLRI) status bits. It specifies the result reporting mechanisms for the Source Controller upon successful operation execution and provides criteria for evaluating reset command status.

children.title: 5.4.4 Discovery Information Management command
children.description: The section details the NVMe Discovery Information Management (DIM) command’s Task (TAS) field

children.title: 5.4.4.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the command completion process and status codes for the Discovery Information Management command, detailing requirements for Extended Discovery Information Entries and error conditions such as "Invalid Discovery Information" (2Fh) and "Insufficient Discovery Resources" (32h). It specifies field applicability for Host and NVM Subsystem entries, providing the technical criteria for successful command execution and troubleshooting NVMe discovery configuration errors.

children.title: 5.4.5 Fabric Zoning Lookup command
children.description: This section details the syntax and execution of the Fabric Zoning Lookup command for retrieving active fiber channel zone configurations. It addresses how to troubleshoot member accessibility, identify zoning enforcement policies, and verify port-level connectivity status within a storage area network (SAN).

children.title: 5.4.5.1 Command Completion
children.description: The section details the NVMe Discovery Information Management command’s completion process, enumerating required fields of Extended Discovery Information Entries (e.g., NQN, TRADDR, TSAS, TEL, NUMEXAT) and the controller’s posting of Admin Completion Queue entries with command‑specific status codes (0x2F Invalid Discovery Information, 0x32 Insufficient Discovery Resources). It answers how the controller validates entry types against the Entity Type (ETYPE) field, what error conditions trigger specific status values, and how completion status is communicated to the host.

children.title: 5.4.6 Fabric Zoning Receive command
children.description: This section details the **Fabric Zoning Receive (FZR)** command protocol within Fibre Channel fabric services, covering frame structure, payload processing, and synchronization states. It answers how management entities retrieve current zoning databases and configuration parameters from the fabric controller to ensure consistency across the SAN.

children.title: 5.4.6.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the completion and data transfer parameters for Fabric Zoning Lookup (FZL) and Fabric Zoning Receive (FZR) commands within the NVMe 2.3 specification. It details the utilization of Zoning Data Keys (ZDK), Data Pointers (DPTR), and Zoning Data Offsets (ZDO) to facilitate precise data structure management between DDCs and CDCs.

children.title: 5.4.7 Fabric Zoning Send command
children.description: This section details the NVMe "Fabric Zoning Send" (FZS) command, specifying technical parameters such as Data Pointer usage, command Dwords, and fragment handling. It answers how a host initiates the transfer of zoning data structures to a Centralized Discovery Controller (CDC) and defines the necessary command field configurations for successful data transmission.

children.title: 5.4.7.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section specifies Command Completion requirements for the Fabric Zoning Send (FZS) command, including Command Dword 12 bit definitions (ZDKC, NUMD) and associated Admin Completion Queue status codes. It provides technical guidance on handling controller-specific abort conditions, such as Invalid Field in Command, and defines error status values for zoning data structure locking, availability, and feature enable states.

children.title: 5.4.8 Manage Exported Namespace command
children.description: This section defines the Fabric Zoning Send (FZS) command structure for NVMe-over-Fabrics, detailing Command Dword 12 bit-fields (ZDKC, NUMD) and specific protocol-level error conditions. It addresses how to configure transaction IDs versus zoning data keys and outlines command-specific status codes used to manage zoning data structure locks and availability on Centralized Discovery Controllers (CDC) versus Discovery Data Controllers (DDC).

children.title: 5.4.8.1 Manage Exported Namespace Management Operations
children.description: This section details the NVMe-oF management operations for dynamically associating and disassociating namespaces with specific hosts. It defines the technical procedures for executing Management Operation identifiers 01h and 02h to configure exported namespace access and controller resource mapping.

children.title: 5.4.8.1.1 Associate Namespace (Management Operation 01h)
children.description: This section outlines the process for associating an Exported Namespace ID (ENSID) with an Underlying Namespace and Exported NVM Subsystem using management operation 01h. It details the required use of the Associate Namespace data structure and clarifies that this operation does not automatically attach the namespace to a controller.

children.title: 5.4.8.1.2 Disassociate Namespace (Management Operation 02h)
children.description: This section describes the Disassociate Namespace management operation (opcode 02h) of the NVMe Manage Exported Namespace command, detailing the required Disassociate Namespace data structure (Exported Namespace ID [ENSID], Exported NVM Subsystem NQN [ENSNQN]), error handling (abort with Command Sequence Error if the namespace is attached), and the associated Allocated Namespace Attribute Changed asynchronous event. It answers how to remove the binding between an Exported Namespace ID and an Exported NVM Subsystem, which fields must be supplied, and what status codes and events are generated.

children.title: 5.4.8.2 Command Completion
children.description: This section details the **Disassociate Namespace** management operation, specifically defining the **Disassociate Namespace Data Structure** and the requirements for removing associations between an **Exported Namespace ID (ENSID)** and an **Exported NVM Subsystem**. It specifies error conditions for commands involving attached namespaces and provides structural definitions for the **ENSID** and **Exported NVM Subsystem NQN** fields.

children.title: 5.4.9 Manage Exported NVM Subsystem command
children.description: Routing description: This section explains the Manage Exported NVM Subsystem command’s Disassociate Namespace (02h) operation, detailing the Disassociate Namespace data structure fields (ENSID, ENSNQN), the procedure for deleting an Exported Namespace ID, validation rules, and error handling (Command Sequence Error) when the namespace is attached to a controller. It answers how to remove an association between an Exported Namespace and an Exported NVM Subsystem, what parameters must be supplied, and under what conditions the command is aborted.

children.title: 5.4.9.1 Manage Exported NVM Subsystem Management Operations
children.description: This section outlines the NVMe-over-Fabrics administrative procedures for managing exported NVM subsystems through specific management operations (01h–04h). It provides technical guidance on executing deletion, access mode modification, and host-level access control (granting or revoking) for exported storage resources.

children.title: 5.4.9.1.2 Change Access Mode (Management Operation 02h)
children.description: This section defines the "Change Access Mode" (Management Operation 02h) for the Manage Exported NVM Subsystem command within the NVMe 2.3 specification. It details the command structure, specifically Command Dword 10 (SEL field) and Data Pointer (DPTR) requirements, to clarify how hosts regulate access states for exported NVM subsystems.

children.title: 5.4.9.1.3 Grant Host Access (Management Operation 03h)
children.description: This section defines the "Grant Host Access" management operation (03h) and the Restricted Access (RA) bit used to toggle between unrestricted and NQN-based whitelist access for Exported NVM Subsystems. It clarifies how controllers enforce host connectivity and handle association requirements when modifying Exported NVM Subsystem access modes.

children.title: 5.4.9.1.4 Revoke Host Access (Management Operation 04h)
children.description: Revoke Host Access (Management Operation 04h) details the Manage Exported NVM Subsystem command workflow for revoking one‑to‑N host permissions from specified Exported NVM Subsystems via designated ports, the required Subsystem Management data structure, reserved Command Dword 10 handling, and disconnect behavior; it enumerates operation‑specific status codes (35h Invalid Host, 36h Invalid NVM Subsystem) and defines how the Command Specific Information field reports the byte offset of failing entries. This section answers how to specify hosts, ports, and subsystems for access revocation, interpret error codes, and locate failure diagnostics in the Error Information Log.

children.title: 5.4.9.2 Command Completion
children.description: This section explains the “Revoke Host Access” management operation (Opcode 04h) of the Manage Exported NVM Subsystem command, detailing required data

children.title: 5.4.10 Manage Exported Port command
children.description: Revoke Host Access operation of the Manage Exported NVM Subsystem command—command Dword 10 usage, Subsystem Management data structure, reserved Management Operation Specific field, status codes 35h Invalid Host, 36h Invalid NVM Subsystem, host‑to‑port revocation scenarios, and error reporting via Command‑Specific Information offset.

children.title: 5.4.10.1 Manage Exported Port Management Operations
children.description: This section defines the management operations for Exported Port configuration, focusing on the Create (01h) and Delete (02h) command opcodes. It specifies the technical procedures and control parameters required to programmatically provision or deprovision virtual port resources within the management interface.

children.title: 5.4.10.1.1 Create (Management Operation 01h)
children.description: Create management operation for Manage Exported Port (NVMe 2.3 §5.4.10.1.1): defines Export

children.title: 5.4.10.1.2 Delete (Management Operation 02h)
children.description: This section details the parameters and error conditions for the **Delete (Management Operation 02h)** command within the NVMe Base Specification. It defines specific requirements for **Exported Port ID (EPID)** handling and prescribes mandatory abort conditions for invalid **Exported NVM Subsystem NQN** identifiers or port uniqueness conflicts.

children.title: 5.4.10.2 Command Completion
children.description: This section details the command completion procedures for the Manage Exported Port (Delete) operation and defines the structure for the Send Discovery Log Page (SDLP) command. It specifies controller completion queue posting requirements, data pointer usage, and Command Dword 10 configurations for reporting Requested Log Page Status (RLPS).

children.title: 5.4.11 Send Discovery Log Page command
children.description: This section defines the Send Discovery Log Page (SDLP) command, specifying how a Centralized Discovery Controller (CDC) transmits discovery log pages to a pull-model Distributed Discovery Controller (DDC). Technical documentation includes data structure requirements for the Data Pointer (DPTR) and Command Dword (CDW) registers, detailing how the Requested Log Page Status (RLPS) field manages log page validity and transmission protocols.

children.title: 6.1 Authentication Receive Command and Response
children.description: This section defines the **Authentication Receive** command within the **NVMe Fabrics Command Set**, specifying its mandatory status, opcode (08h), and data transfer characteristics. It details how this fabric-specific command facilitates secure controller authentication, providing the technical framework for implementing host-to-controller exchange sequences independent of the **CC.EN** state.

children.title: 6.2 Authentication Send Command and Response
children.description: This section defines the **Authentication Send command** structure within the NVMe Base Specification, detailing parameters such as **Security Protocol (SECP)**, **SP Specific (SPSP)** fields, and **Allocation Length (AL)**. It provides the technical requirements for issuing security-related commands and clarifies the controller's response format regarding status completion and queue management.

children.title: 6.3 Connect Command and Response
children.description: This section details the NVMe Connect command and response mechanism used to establish Submission and Completion Queue pairs and associate hosts with controllers. It addresses technical requirements for command identifiers, status signaling, and host-controller connectivity using Host NQN and Host Identifier configurations.

children.title: 6.4 Disconnect Command and Response
children.description: This section details the NVMe over Fabrics **Connect Response** structure, specifically defining **Dword 0** parameters for successful connections versus invalid parameter errors. It specifies requirements for **in-band authentication**, **secure channel establishment**, and **Controller ID (CNTLID)** allocation to determine host-controller compatibility.

children.title: 6.5 Property Get Command and Response
children.description: This section details the NVMe Disconnect command, specifying its Submission Queue Entry format, the Fabrics Command Type (FCTYPE), and the structure of the Disconnect response. It defines the operational requirements for closing I/O queues and explains how the NVM subsystem reports command status and SQ Head Pointer values to the host.

children.title: 6.6 Property Set Command and Response
children.description: This section defines the NVMe-over-Fabrics Property Set and Get command structures, detailing the Submission Queue Entry (SQE) format and response packet layouts. It specifies how hosts manage controller properties, identifies required fields such as Command Identifier (CID) and Status Info (STS), and outlines error handling for invalid property offsets.

children.title: 7.1 Cancel command
children.description: This section defines the NVM Express I/O Command framework, detailing requirements for command submission, Queue management, and controller state readiness (CSTS.RDY). It specifies the structural standards for Submission Queue (SQE) and Completion Queue (CQE) entries, establishing the operational foundation for all supported I/O Command Sets.

children.title: 7.1.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the **Cancel command** execution procedures, specifically detailing **Action Code (ACODE)** parameters for "Single Command" versus "Multiple Command" abort operations. It clarifies controller behavior regarding **NSID** and **CID** validation, error handling, and completion status requirements for terminating I/O submissions.

children.title: 7.2 Flush command
children.description: This section details the `Flush` command, covering critical topics such as buffer clearing, cache invalidation, and data synchronization triggers. It answers questions regarding command execution, status verification, and methods for ensuring persistent storage consistency during system operations.

children.title: 7.2.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section details the Completion Queue Entry Dword 0 format for the NVMe Cancel command, specifically defining the **Commands Eligible for Deferred Abort (CEDA)** and **Commands Aborted (CMDA)** fields. It clarifies how controllers report immediate versus deferred abort status and provides introductory requirements for the NVMe **Flush command** regarding volatile write cache persistence.

children.title: 7.3 I/O Management Receive command
children.description: This section details the I/O Management Receive command, covering operational procedures, data transmission protocols, and command completion status codes. It addresses how the system handles inbound control requests, manages transaction buffers, and notifies hosts upon the successful or failed execution of peripheral communication tasks.

children.title: 7.3.1 I/O Management Receive Operations
children.description: This section defines I/O Management Receive operations, specifically utilizing the Reclaim Unit Handle Status management command (01h) to monitor resource reclamation states. It addresses technical queries regarding state retrieval, unit handle management, and protocol-specific feedback mechanisms for storage subsystem optimization.

children.title: 7.3.1.1 Reclaim Unit Handle Status (Management Operation 01h)
children.description: This section details the **I/O Management Receive command** (Management Operation 01h), defining parameters for **Reclaim Unit Handle Status** data transfers via **PRPs** or **SGLs**. It specifies command structure requirements, **Data Pointer (DPTR)** usage, and rules for handling **Number of Dwords (NUMD)** field length mismatches during controller-to-host data transfers.

children.title: 7.3.2 Command Completion
children.description: This section details **Command Completion** requirements for the **Reclaim Unit Handle Status** data structure in NVMe specification 2.3. It defines error handling protocols—specifically **FDP Disabled** and **Invalid Namespace** status codes—and specifies the transfer behavior for the **Reclaim Unit Handle Status Descriptor list** based on the **Number of Dwords (NUMD)** field.

children.title: 7.4 I/O Management Send command
children.description: This section defines the "I/O Management Receive" command requirements for NVM Express systems supporting Flexible Data Placement (FDP), including invalid namespace handling, NUMD field length constraints, and descriptor structure. It explains how to retrieve Reclaim Unit Handle Status metrics and the formatting of the associated Reclaim Unit Handle Status Descriptor list.

children.title: 7.4.1 I/O Management Send Operations
children.description: This section covers I/O Management Send operations, specifically detailing the Reclaim Unit Handle Update (Management Operation 01h) protocol. It defines the technical requirements and command structures utilized to manage unit handle reclamation within the storage architecture.

children.title: 7.4.1.1 Reclaim Unit Handle Update (Management Operation 01h)
children.description: This section defines the **I/O Management Send** command (Management Operation 01h) used to force a **Reclaim Unit** rotation by updating referenced **Placement Identifiers** to empty units. It addresses how host software manages data placement and storage utilization by explicitly specifying when to transition a writable placement handle to a new, unwritten Reclaim Unit.

children.title: 7.4.2 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the data structure for the **Reclaim Unit Handle Update** command, specifically detailing the `Placement Identifier` list and mandatory error handling for disabled **Flexible Data Placement (FDP)**. It addresses how controllers must process command completion statuses and specifies required return behaviors for I/O Management and reservation-related command operations.

children.title: 7.5 Reservation Acquire command
children.description: This section details the NVMe **Reservation Acquire command**, covering procedures for namespace reservation acquisition, preempting existing reservations, and associated status handling. It defines the technical requirements for controllers to manage access control and handle reservation conflicts within a persistent storage environment.

children.title: 7.5.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the **Reservation Acquire** command parameters for **NVM Express** devices, specifically detailing the **Dispersed Namespace Reservation Support (DISNSRS)** bit and the **Ignore Existing Key (IEKEY)** field. It addresses how hosts signal support for **dispersed namespaces** and identifies the required error handling behaviors for improper command configurations.

children.title: 7.6 Reservation Register command
children.description: This section defines the **Reservation Register** command parameters within the **NVM Express 2.3** specification, specifically detailing the **Dispersed Namespace Reservation Support (DISNSRS)** bit and the **Ignore Existing Key (IEKEY)** field. It establishes mandatory host requirements for reservation management on dispersed namespaces and specifies controller error-handling protocols for configuration mismatches.

children.title: 7.6.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section details the command completion requirements for NVMe reservation operations, specifically defining the New Reservation Key (NRKEY) field for register actions and the structure for Reservation Release commands and data pointers. It clarifies how controllers report status for reservation-related commands and specifies the command dword 10 parameters required to release or clear existing namespace reservations.

children.title: 7.7 Reservation Release command
children.description: This section defines the **Reservation Release command** within the NVM Express 2.3 specification, detailing the usage of **Command Dword 10**, **Data Pointers (DPTR)**, and the **Reservation Release data structure**. It addresses how a host manages the release or clearing of namespace reservations by specifying the required **Reservation Type (RTYPE)** and signaling mechanisms for command completion.

children.title: 7.7.1 Command Completion
children.description: This section defines the Command Completion requirements for NVMe Reservation Release operations and introduces the Reservation Report command mechanism. It details how controllers return namespace registration status data structures, addressing how to interpret multi-registrant data based on Host Identifier selection and NVM subsystem membership.

children.title: 7.8 Reservation Report command
children.description: The section explains the NVMe Reservation Report command (Spec 2.3), covering the Reservation Status data structure, Registrant and Registrant‑Extended fields, handling of dispersed namespaces, 64‑bit vs 128‑bit Host Identifier formats, and command‑completion queue behavior; it answers how a host retrieves registration and reservation status for a namespace and how host‑ID size determines the returned structure.

children.title: 8.1 Common Extended Capabilities
children.description: Section 8.1 details NVMe Common Extended Capabilities, covering technical specifications for namespace management, power optimization, device self-tests, and data placement strategies like Flexible Data Placement (FDP). This section answers how NVMe controllers handle advanced operational features, including command lockdown, live migration, and capacity management, to ensure interoperability and sophisticated storage resource control.

children.title: 8.1.1 Asymmetric Namespace Access Reporting
children.description: This section details the NVMe Asymmetric Namespace Access (ANA) reporting mechanism, focusing on ANA groups, state definitions (Optimized, Non-Optimized, Inaccessible, Persistent Loss, and Change), and associated asynchronous event change notifications. It provides the technical framework for how controllers manage and report path-specific namespace availability and command processing behavior during state transitions.

children.title: 8.1.1.1 Asymmetric Namespace Access Reporting Overview
children.description: This section defines Asymmetric Namespace Access (ANA) reporting, detailing how NVM subsystems communicate varying performance and accessibility characteristics across different controllers and fabric ports. It addresses key topics including shared vs. private namespace access, ANA state reporting requirements, and the technical criteria for symmetric versus asymmetric controller connectivity in NVMe environments.

children.title: 8.1.1.2 ANA Groups
children.description: This section defines **ANA Groups** in the NVMe specification, detailing how namespaces, NVM Sets, and Endurance Groups are grouped to synchronize **asymmetric namespace access states**. It clarifies organizational requirements for **ANAGRPID** uniqueness, group membership, and vendor-specific domain mapping.

children.title: 8.1.1.5 ANA Non-Optimized state
children.description: This section details the **ANA Non-Optimized state** within the NVMe Asymmetric Namespace Access framework, defining how controllers process commands when namespace group access is permitted but sub-optimal in terms of performance or latency. It explains the functional performance expectations, path characteristics, and operational requirements for namespaces transitioning through or residing in this specific non-optimized state.

children.title: 8.1.1.8 ANA Change state
children.description: This section defines the **ANA Inaccessible state** within NVM Express, detailing mandatory command aborts with the **Asymmetric Access Inaccessible** status code and the clearing of **Identify Namespace** capacity fields. It explains how inhibited user data access triggers this state and instructs hosts to transition to **ANA Optimized** or **ANA Non-Optimized** states for valid namespace metadata.

children.title: 8.1.1.9 Asymmetric Namespace Access Change Notifications
children.description: This section details the mechanism for Asymmetric Namespace Access (ANA) state transition notifications, focusing on Asynchronous Event Requests, ANA Change state machine behavior, and Log Page reporting. It addresses how NVMe hosts detect and synchronize with controller-side namespace accessibility shifts within a multiple-controller subsystem configuration.

children.title: 8.1.1.10 Asymmetric Namespace Access States Command Processing Effects
children.description: This section details host requirements for handling ANA Change states, including retry strategies, transition timing, and asynchronous change notification triggers. It addresses how NVMe controllers manage command processing during state transitions and which administrative commands remain unaffected by Namespace Identifier constraints.

children.title: 8.1.2 Asymmetric Namespace Access Reporting – Host Considerations (Informative)
children.description: This section details host-side handling of NVMe Asymmetric Namespace Access (ANA) states, including Optimized, Non-Optimized, Inaccessible, and Persistent Loss path management. It provides technical guidance on event-driven state transitions, asynchronous change reporting, and recovery strategies for "All Paths Down" conditions to ensure robust I/O path failover and controller connectivity.

children.title: 8.1.2.1 Host ANA Normal Operation
children.description: This section defines **NVM Express Asymmetric Namespace Access (ANA)** state impacts on host command execution, specifically detailing behavioral requirements for **Identify**, **Set Features**, and **NSID**-specific abort conditions. It addresses how controller responses—such as **Asymmetric Access Inaccessible**, **Transition**, or **Persistent Loss**—constrain feature management and namespace visibility during state transitions.

children.title: 8.1.2.4 Host ANA Change Notice Operation
children.description: This section defines host routing logic for NVM Express Asymmetric Namespace Access (ANA), detailing how to prioritize commands across ANA Optimized, Non-Optimized, and Inaccessible controller states. It provides technical guidance on multi-controller load balancing and error handling when namespaces become inaccessible.

children.title: 8.1.2.6 All Paths Down Condition
children.description: This section defines the "All Paths Down" condition within the NVMe ANA framework and outlines host requirements for managing Asymmetric Namespace Access Change Notices. It covers technical procedures for updating ANA log pages, handling ANATT timer expirations, and responding to ANAGRPID modifications to maintain command path availability.

children.title: 8.1.3 Boot Partitions
children.description: This section covers the management, read/write protocols, and security configurations for eMMC boot partitions. It addresses technical requirements for data access, firmware updates, and hardware write-protection mechanisms.

children.description: Routing Description: Details on reading the Boot Partition (Section 8.1.3.1) covering ANA (Asymmetric Namespace Access) state changes, ANA Group

children.title: 8.1.3.2 Writing to a Boot Partition
children.description: This section details the NVM Express Boot Partition read procedure, utilizing key controller registers like `CAP.BPS`, `BPINFO`, and `BPMBL` to manage host memory transfers. It defines the technical requirements for initializing transport links, allocating physically contiguous buffers, and executing data copies from the controller to host memory.

children.title: 8.1.3.3 Boot Partition Write Protection
children.description: This section defines the mandatory procedural sequence for updating NVMe Boot Partitions, covering Firmware Image Download, Write Unlocked State transitions, and Firmware Commit operations. It addresses how to modify firmware images, manage write protection status, and handle error reporting for "Boot Partition Write Prohibited" scenarios.

children.title: 8.1.3.3.1 Set Features Boot Partition Write Protection
children.description: This section defines the configuration requirements for NVMe Boot Partition Write Protection, specifically detailing the eligibility criteria for using the Boot Partition Write Protection Config feature. It clarifies technical dependencies between Boot Partitions, Replay Protected Memory Block (RPMB) support, and legacy compliance according to NVM Express Base Specification revision history.

children.title: 8.1.3.3.2 RPMB Boot Partition Write Protection
children.description: This section defines the **Set Features Boot Partition Write Protection** mechanism, detailing the operational states (Write Unlocked, Write Locked, and Write Locked Until Power Cycle) and their persistence behaviors within the NVMe specification. It addresses how hosts manage Boot Partition security, specifically how to transition between states to enable or restrict write access to the controller’s storage segments.

children.title: 8.1.3.3.3 Interactions between Boot Partition Write Protection Mechanism
children.description: This section details the operational states and interactions of the NVMe Boot Partition write protection mechanisms, specifically focusing on RPMB-based locking, Firmware Commit command constraints, and power-cycle reset behaviors. It addresses how hosts manage independent partition accessibility through Device Configuration Block settings and clarifies the regulatory state machine governing these protection methods.

children.title: 8.1.4 Capacity Management
children.description: This section details infrastructure scaling strategies, covering fixed versus variable resource allocation models, throughput optimization, and demand forecasting. It addresses how to maintain operational performance, align capacity with fluctuating workloads, and effectively manage system overhead.

children.title: 8.1.4.1 Overview
